user_title: Head of Change Execution, WPB Private Banking & Wealth Solutions, Asia Pacific

talking_points.headline: DBS targets S$1 trillion wealth AUM by 2030, backs it with a no-RM-required AI trading platform launching mid-August
talking_points.context_html: DBS is targeting S$1 trillion (~US$775bn) in wealth assets by 2030 — a S$400bn jump from 2025 — backed by 600+ new relationship managers and platform engineers, and will launch a redesigned AI-powered wealth platform in mid-August that gives mass-market clients personalised recommendations and lets them execute trades with zero human involvement once they consent. For Adam, this is the clearest signal yet that a top-three APAC wealth competitor is willing to automate the RM relationship itself at scale, not just augment it — directly bearing on how aggressively WPB's own AI and change-execution roadmap needs to move to defend the mass-affluent segment.
source_links: DBS targets US$775bil in wealth assets

talking_points.headline: HSBC Orion becomes first platform live in the Bank of England's Digital Securities Sandbox
talking_points.context_html: HSBC Orion is now the first platform approved by the Bank of England and FCA to operate in the UK's Digital Securities Sandbox, running as depository for tokenised bond issuance including the UK government's DIGIT digital gilt pilot, with a new MOU signed alongside the London Stock Exchange Group on 16 July. It's a genuine first-mover win in institutional digital assets Adam can draw on in board and client conversations — reinforcing that HSBC leads on regulated tokenisation infrastructure, distinct from and ahead of its consumer-facing PayMe stablecoin push.
source_links: HSBC approved for BoE's digital sandbox

talking_points.headline: China's Moonshot AI closes the capability gap with Western frontier models via Kimi K3
talking_points.context_html: Moonshot AI's new Kimi K3 — a 2.8-trillion-parameter open-weight model — claims performance competitive with Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and ahead of Claude Opus 4.8 and OpenAI's GPT-5.6 on several benchmarks, at roughly half the per-task cost of Opus 4.8. The pace at which open-weight Chinese labs are closing the capability and price gap matters directly for WPB's own AI vendor strategy: Wealth Intelligence runs on OpenAI today, and a widening field of credible, cheaper alternatives strengthens both negotiating leverage and the case for periodically re-testing model choice rather than treating the current vendor stack as fixed.
source_links: Moonshot AI's Kimi K3 rivals OpenAI, Anthropic

Competitor Intelligence.title: DBS targets S$1 trillion in wealth AUM by 2030, to launch AI-powered self-service wealth platform in mid-August
Competitor Intelligence.summary: DBS Group set a target to grow wealth assets under management to S$1 trillion (~US$775bn) by 2030 — up roughly S$400bn from S$632bn at end-2025 — and will hire more than 600 relationship managers and platform engineers across Singapore, Hong Kong, China, India, Indonesia and Taiwan by end-2028. The bank will also launch a redesigned AI-powered wealth platform in mid-August offering mass-market customers personalised investment recommendations, chatbot support, and the ability to execute trades with no human involvement once a client consents.

HSBC News.title: HSBC Orion becomes first platform approved to go live in Bank of England's Digital Securities Sandbox
HSBC News.summary: HSBC Orion became the first platform approved by the Bank of England and FCA to go live in the UK's Digital Securities Sandbox, operating as a Digital Securities Depository for tokenised bond issuance including the government's DIGIT digital gilt pilot. HM Treasury separately confirmed on 16 July that HSBC and the London Stock Exchange Group have signed an MOU to build a bilateral Digital Securities Depository link, with DIGIT pilot issuance now expected in early 2027.

AI & Technology.title: China's Moonshot AI narrows the gap with US frontier labs with 2.8-trillion-parameter Kimi K3
AI & Technology.summary: Beijing-based Moonshot AI released Kimi K3, a 2.8-trillion-parameter open-weight model it calls the largest of its kind, claiming performance competitive with Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and ahead of Claude Opus 4.8 and OpenAI's GPT-5.6 on several benchmarks. Priced at roughly half the per-task cost of Opus 4.8, the release underscores how quickly Chinese open-weight labs are closing the capability and price gap with US frontier AI providers.

AI & Technology.title: Buy-side GenAI survey: 77% now run organisation-wide deployments, but broker-data licensing is the top adoption barrier
AI & Technology.summary: A Substantive Research/Aiera survey of 35 major global asset managers found 77% now have organisation-wide generative AI deployments in place, with broker research cited as the most valuable input to receive as machine-readable feeds. Broker and data-licensing restrictions were the top-cited barrier to deeper adoption (69%), ahead of compliance and entitlement concerns (54%), pointing to a data-access bottleneck that will shape how fast agentic AI scales across the industry.
